nodes.title: OpenSpec: worker-findings-dashboard
nodes.description: Execution tracker for openspec/changes/worker-findings-dashboard. Adds a Worker Findings tab to BdHealthPanel and a provenance chip to TaskListItem, backed by a new list_worker_findings Tauri command.

nodes.title: 1.1: Create WorkerFinding struct in workers.rs
nodes.description: openspec/changes/worker-findings-dashboard/tasks.md §1.1. Create `src-tauri/src/commands/workers.rs` with `WorkerFinding { issue_id: String, title: String, worker: String, priority: i32, status: String, notes_first_line: String, created_at: String }`, deriving `serde::Serialize`, `serde::Deserialize`, and `specta::Type`.

nodes.title: 1.2: Add parse_worker_from_notes helper
nodes.description: openspec/changes/worker-findings-dashboard/tasks.md §1.2. Add a private helper `parse_worker_from_notes(notes: &str) -> Option<String>` using regex `^Auto-filed by ruflo-(?P<worker>[a-z0-9-]+) on `. Reuse the existing `regex` crate (already a transitive dep via `sqlx`/`tauri`; add it explicitly to `src-tauri/Cargo.toml` if not present).

nodes.title: 1.3: Add list_worker_findings Tauri command
nodes.description: openspec/changes/worker-findings-dashboard/tasks.md §1.3. Add `list_worker_findings(project_path, registry) -> Result<Vec<WorkerFinding>, String>` Tauri command that: (a) acquires the Dolt SQL pool for `project_path` via the existing `ProjectRegistry`; (b) runs the SQL query filtering notes LIKE 'Auto-filed by ruflo-%' AND status IN ('open','in_progress') AND issue_type != 'deleted' ORDER BY created_at DESC; (c) parses worker via the helper; (d) skips rows where the regex fails to match.

nodes.title: 1.4: Add pub mod workers to commands/mod.rs
nodes.description: openspec/changes/worker-findings-dashboard/tasks.md §1.4. Add `pub mod workers;` to `src-tauri/src/commands/mod.rs`.

nodes.title: 1.5: Register list_worker_findings in lib.rs
nodes.description: openspec/changes/worker-findings-dashboard/tasks.md §1.5. Register `commands::workers::list_worker_findings` in `src-tauri/src/lib.rs` inside both `tauri_specta::collect_commands!` and `tauri::generate_handler!`.

nodes.title: 2.1: Unit test parse_worker_from_notes
nodes.description: openspec/changes/worker-findings-dashboard/tasks.md §2.1. Unit test `parse_worker_from_notes` for the canonical prefix, hyphenated worker names, mid-string occurrence (must NOT match — anchored to start), and missing space before `on` (must NOT match).

nodes.title: 2.2: Integration test against temp Dolt registry
nodes.description: openspec/changes/worker-findings-dashboard/tasks.md §2.2. Integration-style test against a temp Dolt registry: seed three issues — one with the canonical notes prefix, one with a `ruflo:<worker>` label but no matching notes, one with a different `Auto-filed by` author prefix. Assert only the first appears in the returned vec and the `worker` token is correctly parsed.

nodes.title: 2.3: Test closed issues and deleted rows are excluded
nodes.description: openspec/changes/worker-findings-dashboard/tasks.md §2.3. Test that closed issues are excluded (status filter) and that `issue_type = 'deleted'` rows are excluded.

nodes.title: 2.4: Test created_at DESC ordering for multiple findings
nodes.description: openspec/changes/worker-findings-dashboard/tasks.md §2.4. Test that `created_at DESC` ordering is preserved when multiple findings share the same worker.

nodes.title: 3.1: Regenerate bindings.ts with WorkerFinding and listWorkerFindings
nodes.description: openspec/changes/worker-findings-dashboard/tasks.md §3.1. Run `bun tauri build` (or the existing specta codegen script) to regenerate `src/bindings.ts` with `WorkerFinding` and the `listWorkerFindings` command stub.

nodes.title: 3.2: Add listWorkerFindings wrapper in src/ipc.ts
nodes.description: openspec/changes/worker-findings-dashboard/tasks.md §3.2. Add `listWorkerFindings(projectPath: string): Promise<WorkerFinding[]>` wrapper in `src/ipc.ts` using the existing `unwrap(commands.listWorkerFindings(projectPath))` idiom.

nodes.title: 4.1: Add parseWorkerProvenance helper in src/lib/worker-findings.ts
nodes.description: openspec/changes/worker-findings-dashboard/tasks.md §4.1. Add `src/lib/worker-findings.ts` exporting `parseWorkerProvenance(notes: string | null | undefined): { worker: string; firstLine: string } | null` using the same anchored regex as the Rust helper. Document the coupling to `on-finding.sh` and to `parse_worker_from_notes`.

nodes.title: 4.2: Unit test parseWorkerProvenance
nodes.description: openspec/changes/worker-findings-dashboard/tasks.md §4.2. Add a unit test under `src/lib/__tests__/worker-findings.test.ts` mirroring the Rust cases from task 2.1 plus a `null` / `undefined` / empty-string trio.

nodes.title: 5.1: Add provenance chip to TaskListItem
nodes.description: openspec/changes/worker-findings-dashboard/tasks.md §5.1. Confirm whether `Task` row payload already carries `notes`. If yes: in `TaskListItem.tsx`, compute `const provenance = parseWorkerProvenance(task.notes)` and render a chip before the existing `visibleLabels.map(...)` chip row. If no: hoist a `Map<issue_id, worker>` from a `useWorkerFindingsLookup(projectPath)` hook in the parent `TaskList` and pass `workerForRow` as an optional prop.

nodes.title: 5.2: Update TaskListItemProps Pick set to include notes
nodes.description: openspec/changes/worker-findings-dashboard/tasks.md §5.2. Update the `TaskListItemProps` `task` `Pick<>` set to include `notes` if the inline path is chosen.

nodes.title: 5.3: Style the provenance chip identically to label chips
nodes.description: openspec/changes/worker-findings-dashboard/tasks.md §5.3. Style the chip identically to other label chips: `text-[10px] px-1.5 py-0 rounded font-mono leading-4 max-w-24 truncate ${labelChipClass('worker:' + worker)}`. Use the `worker:` prefix so it picks up `LABEL_CHIP_COLORS.worker`. Apply `formatLabel('worker:' + worker)` so the chip text shows just the worker name.

nodes.title: 5.4: Add title and aria-label to provenance chip
nodes.description: openspec/changes/worker-findings-dashboard/tasks.md §5.4. Add `title={provenance.firstLine}` so the full prefix is visible on hover, and `aria-label={"Filed by ruflo-" + provenance.worker}`.

nodes.title: 5.5: Ensure provenance chip does not count toward +N overflow
nodes.description: openspec/changes/worker-findings-dashboard/tasks.md §5.5. Ensure the chip does NOT count toward the `task.labels.length > 2` `+N` overflow indicator.

nodes.title: 6.1: Create WorkerFindingsPanel with grouped findings query
nodes.description: openspec/changes/worker-findings-dashboard/tasks.md §6.1. Create `src/components/health/WorkerFindingsPanel.tsx` that uses `useQuery(['worker-findings', projectPath], () => listWorkerFindings(projectPath))`, groups findings by `worker` (preserve insertion order from DESC-ordered fetch), and renders one section per worker.

nodes.title: 6.2: Render worker section headers with count and severity breakdown
nodes.description: openspec/changes/worker-findings-dashboard/tasks.md §6.2. Each section header shows: worker name (with teal chip styling), total count, and a severity breakdown line `(1 critical, 4 high, 2 medium)` computed from `priority` via `priorityToSeverity()`. Hide severity counts of 0.

nodes.title: 6.3: Render finding rows with navigation to openPinned
nodes.description: openspec/changes/worker-findings-dashboard/tasks.md §6.3. Each finding row shows: issue ID, `P{priority}` styled via `PRIORITY_STYLE`, title (truncate), and `created_at` short-date. Clicking the row calls the existing workspace `openPinned(issue_id)` — match keyboard/Enter contract used by `TaskListItem`.

nodes.title: 6.4: Render empty state for WorkerFindingsPanel
nodes.description: openspec/changes/worker-findings-dashboard/tasks.md §6.4. Empty state: when `findings.length === 0`, render `"No worker findings. Run /audit, /ruflo-loop testgaps, or /ruflo-cost to generate some."` centred with the same neutral-600 chrome as the existing empty state.

nodes.title: 6.5: Add loading and error states to WorkerFindingsPanel
nodes.description: openspec/changes/worker-findings-dashboard/tasks.md §6.5. Loading state: spinner matching the existing `CheckSection` spinner. Error state: red error pill with the error string.

nodes.title: 7.1: Add checks/findings tab strip to BdHealthPanel
nodes.description: openspec/changes/worker-findings-dashboard/tasks.md §7.1. In `BdHealthPanel.tsx`, introduce `useState<'checks' | 'findings'>('checks')` and render a small tab strip below the existing header. Style: underline tabs, `text-neutral-300` inactive, `text-neutral-100 border-b border-neutral-100` active. Strip is `border-b border-neutral-800`.

nodes.title: 7.2: Extract ChecksBody sub-component or inline conditional
nodes.description: openspec/changes/worker-findings-dashboard/tasks.md §7.2. Move the existing body (`bdNotFound`, `projectNotConnected`, `allPassed`, and the `CHECKS.map(...)` render) into a `<ChecksBody />` sub-component or inline conditional, gated by `tab === 'checks'`.

nodes.title: 7.3: Render WorkerFindingsPanel on findings tab
nodes.description: openspec/changes/worker-findings-dashboard/tasks.md §7.3. When `tab === 'findings'`, render `<WorkerFindingsPanel />`.

nodes.title: 7.4: Gate Re-run button to checks tab only
nodes.description: openspec/changes/worker-findings-dashboard/tasks.md §7.4. The `Re-run` button is gated to `tab === 'checks'`. When the findings tab is active, the button is hidden.

nodes.title: 7.5: Verify auto-run-on-mount effect is unaffected by tab state
nodes.description: openspec/changes/worker-findings-dashboard/tasks.md §7.5. Verify the auto-run-on-mount effect (`useEffect` keyed on `[project, hasRun, runChecks]`) still triggers only the checks side and is not affected by tab state.

nodes.title: 8.1: cargo test workers::tests passes
nodes.description: openspec/changes/worker-findings-dashboard/tasks.md §8.1. `cargo test -p beadspec_lib workers::tests` (or whichever crate hosts `commands::workers`) passes with all four backend tests.

nodes.title: 8.2: bun tsc --noEmit passes after bindings regeneration
nodes.description: openspec/changes/worker-findings-dashboard/tasks.md §8.2. `bun tsc --noEmit` passes after bindings regeneration.

nodes.title: 8.3: bun test worker-findings.test.ts passes
nodes.description: openspec/changes/worker-findings-dashboard/tasks.md §8.3. `bun test src/lib/__tests__/worker-findings.test.ts` passes.

nodes.title: 8.4: Manual: verify provenance chip in task list
nodes.description: openspec/changes/worker-findings-dashboard/tasks.md §8.4. Manual: `bun tauri dev`, trigger `/audit` (or seed a Beads issue with matching notes), confirm the new chip appears on its row in the task list and the issue appears under `ruflo-security-audit` in the Worker findings tab.

nodes.title: 8.5: Manual: verify closed findings disappear from panel
nodes.description: openspec/changes/worker-findings-dashboard/tasks.md §8.5. Manual: close the seeded issue via the task detail panel, confirm it disappears from the Worker findings tab within one task-cache refresh cycle.

nodes.title: 8.6: Manual: verify Checks tab auto-runs and Re-run hidden on findings tab
nodes.description: openspec/changes/worker-findings-dashboard/tasks.md §8.6. Manual: confirm the `Checks` tab still auto-runs on mount and the `Re-run` button is hidden on the `Worker findings` tab.

nodes.title: 8.7: openspec validate worker-findings-dashboard passes
nodes.description: openspec/changes/worker-findings-dashboard/tasks.md §8.7. `openspec validate worker-findings-dashboard` passes with zero errors.
